summary: Remote ischemic postconditioning has not been shown to provide a clinically significant protection against ischemic injury during percutaneous coronary intervention based on recent high-quality randomized trials. The absence of demonstrated efficacy despite plausible biological theories limits its adoption as standard practice for this setting.
